_about: Four claims + scopes that each exercise one honesty branch of the neuroclaim_compile MCP primitive. Each entry maps 1:1 onto the neuroclaim_compile tool arguments (claim_text, population/modality/datasets/workflow_family, optional plan, extraction_confidence, backend, run_sensitivity). `expected_branch` records the honesty property the claim is chosen to prove; it is documentation, not a tool argument.

claims.claim_text: Amygdala activation is greater for fearful than neutral faces.
claims._note: A forward-activation contrast (no reverse-inference marker). Should compile to an evidential verdict; the MANDATORY sensitivity sweep must have run (sensitivity.ran == true).

claims.claim_text: The dorsolateral prefrontal cortex engages working memory.
claims._note: A 'region X engages cognition Y' claim backed only by forward-convergence evidence. P(region|cognition) != P(cognition|region) (Poldrack 2006), so a clean support must be capped to `qualified` with a reverse-inference caveat.

claims.claim_text: An EEG dataset can be analyzed with an fMRI first-level GLM to localize face-selective activation.
claims._note: A type-incoherent plan (an EEG dataset routed into a volumetric-MNI fMRI GLM). The deterministic typecheck must short-circuit to status `ill_typed` with verdict_basis `structural` BEFORE any evidence layer is consulted.

claims.claim_text: Insular cortex functional connectivity predicts trait anxiety severity.
claims._note: The evidence backend is unreachable at query time (e.g. Neo4j offline). The compiler must ABSTAIN with a typed degraded report: status `unresolved`, degraded == true, evidence_verdict null — an unavailable backend is NEVER laundered into a supported verdict.
